reviews.review_text:
Keep driving.
No fence or foliage or barrier of any kind to block the noise from the highway. Road trains passing ALL night.
Facilities are terrible. Spiders in the toilet cubicles and millipedes in the showers. Kitchen (or what passes for one) hadn't been cleaned in a long time.

Urgh....where to start? The smells, the sights, the sounds?  The constant road noise drove me to use ear plugs every night just to sleep. The park itself needs a major overhaul, the gardens are in dire need of maintenance and no security fencing whatsoever between you and the road. Ablution blocks are awful, insects, bugs and mould in abundance.  The stench of sewage and unemptied feminine hygiene bins on a warm day OMG!  Rainy days saw mud and dirt trekked in by guests and no attempts made to stay the flow of mud. The owners of the park (not the managers) need a kick up the bum. I personally cleaned the ladies ablutions just to make them bearable! ICK....avoid at all costs for your own sanity!!

Unfortunately it is on the highway which makes it noisy but I felt unsafe because of the lack of fences and people coming in out using the showers which there is only 2 the Web page needs an update(it shows a pool.which is long gone
Good for 2 nites
Very nice people, we were up the back, fairly quiet. Not in the city, but not far out. Cooking facilities, good shower.

Nice hot showers but tonnes of millipedes on the floors and walls which made for an uncomfortable and odd smelling shower. They had 2 washing machines, both broken when we arrived, one was repaired that day but only cold washes available for $4. Staff were friendly but definitely overpriced.

We stayed in a powered caravan site the power box worked of and on even though they knew about it had not been fixed like a lot of things in the park were very run down. Cleaning of toilets etc were not up to scratch the worm (fly larvae) were all over toilets and showers and know for a fact the RAC camp down the road keep their s under control. Noisey pipes rattling. The place was very depressing and would never go back or recommend to anyone else.

The receptionist and workers were very friendly and kind. The only thing that was a bit iffy was we tried to call the office a couple times within 3 days before we arrived, we have  left a voice message but no answer and no call back. On the day of arrival we managed to get a hold of the office. Besides that everything was good.
Lounges uncomfortble water not hot needs upgrading badly
Location, parking , support from the office after hours , clean and spacious

This park has potential but needs some TLC and basics done. We had 3 nights in ensuite site. The ensuite looked like it needed a good clean, the shower head was spurting water onto roof, which we told them and rectified immediately. The taps on sink didnt fit properly  and kept falling off, again told them. |I get its old but just purchasing new bathmat from Kmart would be a  good start as bathmat looked dirty. |Placing more than one roll toilet paper in the cupboard. Having a seat or small bench to place clothes on would be helpful. |The camp kitchen was ok - clean with what we used. The BBQ on first night no gas - again we let them know. |The website shows a pool in photos but there is no pool its been removed so should rectify this as misleading. |The outdoor table near camp kitchen is all rusted so just replacing this... |We didnt look at laundry or the shared amenities block. |Staff in office the Lady on checkin was lovely and helpful. |We were right next to road but that was not a bother to us. |

Ein solider Campingplatz für kurzen Aufenthalt super. Hatten einen Stellplatz mit Strom Ensuite. Bad war etwas älter, aber sauber. Camp Kitchen sauber und alles funktioniert. Spielplatz besteht aus einer Schaukel, auch etwas in die Jahre gekommen. Location ist direkt an der Straße, aber hat nicht gestört auch nicht nachts.

Such a beautiful place kids enjoyed it..
Loved the personal shower&toilets.
Awesome first experience in Esperance, thank you so much to managers  Lisa&partner for a great stay.

Definitely be staying here again.

Stayed here for 4 nights with our campervan and really enjoyed our stay. The amenities did the job, and the lady behind the reception was lovely and accommodating for us when we arrived later on. Just outside esperance a 5 minute drive so fine location. Thankyou!
Absolutely avoid until you hear their significant plumping issues have been resolved and more cleaners have been employed.

We paid approximately $700 for a cabin for a 3‑night stay. I genuinely wanted to enjoy this place, and the staff at reception were lovely. It was also the only accommodation available at the last minute. Unfortunately, the cabin itself was extremely poorly maintained, and several issues made our stay very disappointing:

1. Wi‑Fi – Although advertised, the Wi‑Fi was essentially non‑existent. Both of us were using iPhones, so it was not a device issue.
2. Television – The TV was described as a smart TV, but due to the unusable Wi‑Fi, we couldn’t access any streaming services. Even the standard TV signal was so weak that we couldn’t watch regular channels.
3. Cleanliness – There was a cockroach inside one of the ceiling light diffusers.
4. Kitchen surfaces – The benchtops were dusty and visibly dirty. When I ran my finger across the table, it came away black with dust.
5. Plumbing – The kitchen tap leaked and sprayed water from behind, and the bathroom tap dripped continuously.
6. Bathroom – The shower was grimy, and the exhaust fan in the bathroom was black & full of dust, was putrid.

I understand that the property is older, but “old” should never mean dirty or neglected. Older cabins can absolutely be clean, well‑maintained, and comfortable — this one was far from it.

I would not stay here again, and the accommodation urgently needs a thorough deep clean and a significant update.
Not very clean and not cleaned regularly. Good laundry Next to a noisy road

Camping fonctionnel mais un peu désuet. Le choix ancien de mettre des toilettes et douches pour chaque emplacement s'avère difficile quand plusieurs personnes l'occupent car il faudrait un nettoyage permanent. Donc la vétusté a son poids. Autre inconvénient la proximité du périphérique et le bruit du traffic des road trains.
A par ça ça reste correct pour une ville très touristique.
Love the place just had a beautiful peaceful feeling.yeah its a little older but clean and tidy, lovely shady trees and green grass make the place really enjoyable.
If you like not been able to sleep due to heavy traffic and like filthy en-suites then this is the place for you .Im from the uk and the en suite would be demolished.

What a disappointment – paid for a premium cabin and received a budget one.
Money over service.

We booked and paid in full 7 months in advance for a wheelchair-accessible cabin and a second cabin next to it so we could assist the person using the wheelchair. This arrangement was clearly confirmed on our invoice.

We had a late check-in and collected the keys from the outside lock box without issue. However, we were extremely disappointed to discover that we had been placed in a budget cabin as far away as possible from the wheelchair-accessible cabin, making our stay unnecessarily difficult.

The budget “family” cabin (Cabin No. 14) had very limited facilities—only an air fryer, kettle, and microwave. Because there were no proper cooking facilities, we had to constantly move back and forth to the wheelchair-accessible cabin just to prepare and eat meals.

Over our three-day stay, we did not see anyone from management—no follow-up to check how the late check-in went and no concern for the wellbeing of the guest in the wheelchair-accessible cabin. Once payment was taken, there was no customer service at all.

The wheelchair-accessible cabin itself is outdated but does at least offer enough space for wheelchair use. Unfortunately, being placed so far apart completely defeated the purpose of booking well in advance.

Advice to future guests: make sure you receive exactly what you paid for, even if it’s confirmed on your invoice. We strongly recommend avoiding Cabin No. 14.

A very disappointing experience.

I stayed at this camp site for 6 days over new years, it did not meet my expectations at all.
The bathrooms closest to our site that we were expected to use, was an ensuite block they put portable toilets on, the ensuite on the block was rarely cleaned and the bin was overflowing for days, but if you want to walk to the main amenities, there was dead bugs all around the sink that no one had cleaned away for days, no one cleaned the shower after themself with the single mop and bucket with smelly dirty water that clearly hadn’t been replaced, if the campsite expects us to clean the main bathrooms ourself (which only has 2 showers) at least replace the mop water after it’s been used for days. I went in there one evening to go to the toilet, and there was a poo on the ground next to a toilet, I know that’s not on the campsite so I let a worker know about it and I was told it’d be cleaned, the next morning it was still there, and that afternoon it had been quickly picked up but was still clearly there and not cleaned, next day I was going to clean my shower before using it because the last person clearly hadn’t cleaned it and it was hardly bearable being in that bathroom after that, the mop water stank and I can only imagine what that water was used for.
I actually went in there 2 times the mop wasn’t even there and the bathroom was filthy.
The campsite kitchen had almost nothing in it to use apart from a portable stove top and 2 mini fridges.
The lady in reception is not very easy to approach about any issues, the names of our booking got mixed up and it caused us a lot of hassle with our payment.

WOULD NOT RECOMMEND!!

Had issues with payment when we arrived. I had spoken to who was in charge over the phone and sent an email as per there request of the changes made to my booking, I had received an email reply saying that the changes had been made. On arrival it was clear that the changes had not been made, they mixed up our payments and the lady at the front desk did not apologise for the mix up or was accommodating. She had said that it was our problem and took no accountability. On top of that, there were three potable toilets and an ensuite bathroom we were expected to use, the bathroom was not clean, dead bugs everywhere and the showers pressure kept changing. When I went to use it, there was only one drop of water coming from the shower head so I had to use the bathroom block in the middle of the campsite. Those bathrooms were not any better. Dead bugs everywhere also, it was dirty and felt as though they were never cleaned the whole time we were there, (we stayed for 6 night). There were only two toilets and two showers. After showering, I still didn’t feel clean due to the state of the bathroom. I will not be coming back. We would have mentioned it to the owner but as stated before, she was not very approachable.

Came to this campsite with our family who live in Perth after 10 hour drive what a disappointment 😡 we was placed near a main road traffic so loud 😡for the price very disappointing would not recommend

We family of four children stayed in their 2 brick house, spacious rooms, enough amenities. Living rooms sofas are very old and dirty, kitchen have enough utensils. Toilets were clean but found few cockroaches.Silent atmosphere. Not luxurious just basis for 3-4 days trip with family.
Great park, great staff
Friendly staff nice spot.
We stayed on a powered site in this park.

Staff very friendly Jacinta organised a late check in for us. The Park was very clean and ablution block was nice and tidy, showers hot and water flow was good. We stayed there on a powered site. It wasgrain season so the trucks were running constantly but other than that it was a nice little park. No pool but great set of swings and stuff for the kiddies.
Stayed here for 2  nights. Nice park with good sized caravan sites. Close to all sights and facilities  in Esperance.  Friendly helpful  staff.

The manager, Jacinta is so accommodating and did everything to meet our requirements. I was particularly impressed with the welcoming, friendly feel of Acclaim, pet friendly, clean, good sized caravan sites and professional staff. I look forward to returning soon.

Sehr freundlicher Empfang. Top Gastgeber! Die Sanitäranlagen wurden sanft renoviert und sind sauber. Unser Stellplatz lag an der mehr oder weniger stark befahrenen Strasse. In der Nacht war es jedoch ruhig und tagsüber waren  wir eh unterwegs. Wir waren 3 Nächte hier und haben uns wohl gefühlt.

Stayed on an ensuite site at the front of the park very clean, New managers going all out to fix and improve things, lovey friendly staff and will definitely stay again, We wish them the best in thier endeavours
Amazing stay. New managers Jacinta and Mark are great. A breath of fresh air how well they are changing the place. Much needed and so glad to see it happening, as is everyone else :)
Great stay, friendly staff and very helpful. Will be back thank you :)

The management of this caravan park are absolutely amazing people Ken and Trish welcomed us from the time I open the office door the park is tidy the cabins are cosy had everything u need pet friendly we had the best stay with friendly people so if u ever need a great caravan park in esprence with great management this is the one to go to  Would like to say to Ken and Trish thank u so much for making our stay so very welcoming and everything u guys did for us while we were here 100% best management ever
Trish is a very helpful and friendly service manager.
Nice and green

This park needs some $ spent on it. We had an ensuite site at the back of the park. It was a tight fit for our modest camper trailer. The pool no longer exists and was filled with pinebark. The shower dripped constantly and there were plumbing issues with the toilet. Repeated flushes were no guarantee that everything would be gone. I spoke to the office, who advised me that there were long-standing issues with the sewerage in that part of the park. Ok, but why do campers have to suffer? It was very sheltered, a blessing with the winds in Esperance. Hopefully, the problems will get sorted.

The caretakers of this property are just lovely!!
The park is a beautiful little park in esperance but feels like out in the country,
I would definitely come back and I recommend it!
Dated amenities but they work and they're clean!!

Friendly professional staff.
Great facilities.
Will be going back for sure.
Spider everywhere, walls and doors are covered with dirty marks.

2 bedroom cabin - extremely affordable, full kitchen, clean - January 2025.
The couple who look after this holiday park are so kind and helpful. We had issues with the booking (not from their side) and they helped us sort through it in the most friendly manner.
Then we had a couple of other concerns and questions, these were also dealt with promptly and in the most professional way. We were super happy with the service and to us that is extremely important.

Thank you!

This park should be renamed. The last resort caravan park of shattered dreams.
Please, I implore you exhaust every other option in Esperance and surrounds before booking this park.
This is an old park but it’s fine as a base. A bit noisy during the day as there’s an industrial area across the road. It’s ok at night though. We had site 29 which was excellent for our 9m motorhome

We were coming across the Nullarbor and this was the only park in Esperance with a caravan site available! Don’t listen to the negative reviews as it’s awesome. Right from the phone call to checking in the managers and team are all so lovely and helpful. They are out of town which is nice too. Lovely large shady sites!! Beautiful hot showers! We also did a load of much needed washing and lots of space to hang out in sun and breeze!! Thank you 🙏

I quite like our site 29, it’s got a lot of privacy and vegetation around it and a slight elevation over the rest of the park, but let me explain. The park is in the industrial area and Harbour road (just out front) is pretty busy with trucks and traffic during the daytime, night time is pretty quiet. The amenities block is fairly dated and the toilets are the type that take several flushes every time to clear the load. It’s reasonably close to supermarkets and the beaches are an easy bicycle ride.
Staff was friendly but everything was dirty. Paid $58 for an en-suite site. Kitchen and bbq area are just sad. And you can hear people arriving and leaving all night. Even the police (?) just weird

This has got to be one of the worst, no, the worst, caravan park I have had the displeasure to stay in.
The mens shower cubicles are covered in black mould (No exageration) and the floors look like that haven't seen a mop or scrubbing brush for a long time.
They need to be demolished.
Stay here at your own peril.
Further, there is no dump point in the park.  The nearest is a 6 minute drive away.
We would have moved to another park other than Esperance is full (Labour Day Long Weekend) and who can afford to pay for accomodation twice.

The caravan park has so much potential but, what will it take for owners/manager to actually listen!
I booked a powered site for two nights to see if I wanted to stay longer, but......
There are only 2 showers/toilets for women and the same number for men
This is made much worse by the state of the amenities. DIRTY...DIRTY..DIRTY!! I was so repulsed by the state of the bathrooms but had no other choice.
Who is doing the cleaning here, the guests?? The dirty mop left in the bathroom, filth gathered in the corners and dead bug in exactly the same spot for three days says it all!
No effort made as it's a niche market and they will get bookings regardless.
If there is a sad story behind the pool no longer being there, DO something about it. Maybe take the photo of the pool down from your website and make over that whole area to make it seem like you actually care.
Their reply was the same, putting the onus on me for not advising them of any issues I had, instead of taking action to fix the problems that have been mentioned in reviews over the last few months.
"CLEAN UP THE ABLUTIONS"

Been on the road 16 months. Probably the top three WORST places I’ve stayed.
Mens facilities are in a state that I really didn’t want to touch anything.
The whole joint needs a refurb and some serious cleaning up.
Pool has been filled with dirt.. other sites looked flat, unfortunately ours had massive tree roots under the existing ground mat the we loved tripping over.

😳

Very basic caravan park with all powered sites. Small kitchen and bathroom areas with only 2 washing machines for the whole site. Very close to Esperence foreshore and not far from the national park. Other sites in the area are cheaper and better equipped

Older style cabin but was clean. Staff friendly.
Advised management of a few shortfalls and it was taken on board and appreciated. They plan to upgrade the kitchen facilities, she showed me personally what their plan is and appears that will happen very shortly. Quiet park, close to main road, but didn’t effect us.
Not fancy, but cheaper than other parks.

We got there on Saturday 2pm the office suppose to be open, but it wasn’t, so I’ve called the manager and he answer with no kindness at all, costumer service 0. He said I should follow instructions from the message, I informed him that no messaged was received he asked me to come to check was wrong, it was their mistake no one sent us message.
The caravan it was really old nothing like the photos, our site was literally in front of the road with a lot of noise 24h.
Day 3 we left the caravan 10am and we came back 3, our caravan was damaged inside and outside. Someone hit the cord holding annex and pull it till break the cord, ripped the annexe and damage inside structure. Someone was drifting with the cord stuck for long everything breaking and no one saw it ande listen it (our caravan was really close to the office)
We called the manager again not helpful he just gave us a incident report call the police and close his office, he didn’t stay on the place, he didn’t assist us try to fix inside to us sleep one more night, he even checked if the police came. We need a ladder we try to reach him calling him and he didn’t answer.

Old building, with rat traps in the cabin. I suggest this place only if you have your  caravan, avoid the cabins they're old and badly maintained

EDIT: if the traps are for prevention only, please specify it. It really gives out a bad vibe. other camping sites do not have any rat traps.

Camp-site 4 ensuite, caravan, we stayed 26/12  to 3/01/25
Fabulous sites,  great servicing of all amenities
Bins were emptied up to 3 times a day
Yes not on the foreshore but literally 3 mins drive away
All staff were super helpful
We had been dubious about booking based on previous reviews but we will be back !
Great park
Good place to stay in for a short period. Cabin price is acceptable but a bit old! No swimming pool any longer!

Acclaim pine grove holiday park - on a studio room with private bathroom. Only for this bathroom I made booking this park as my choice. I realised on day 1 stay that it is a worst decision I have taken.

The toilets and the kitchen attached in the booking.com website were entirely different from what you get on the site. While I checked with the management, the lady was really sweet and she arranged us additional stove. That was the only sweetest part ever in my 5 day stay.

The toilets were damp smell. Draining of water in the shower was like drop by drop. Could not shower for two days. Spiders everywhere the toilets and near the bin. Used shared bathrooms for 2 days.

Pros: it is not a big kitchen but u will have all the basic utensils and appliances like hot plate mini oven, toaster, kettle and microwave oven with a decent sized refrigerator.

Cons: common kitchen was not that clean and great especially the barbecue grill so fishy smell was not cleaned at all ages.

No swimming pool and this was the biggest disappointment for my kid.

Play area for kids only one swing.

The site is located near to the main road and it was bit noisy but we did not mind it much.

Overall my experience was terrible. Over priced $160 per day. Even if I get a big discount priced I would not recommend to stay for 1 night as well.

Great park. Friendly and helpful managers who actually care about guests and their needs. Lots of shady sites,clean ablution blocks and close to town but not too close to the hussle and bussle. Highly recommend 👌

We stayed one night. We had a late night arrival and called the owner with one of the numbers on the board in front of the reception. He told us to send him a message with the Reg. Number of the vehicle, but the number he told us was not able to receive msg‘s. The second call he was really unfriendly and was not able to note down the reg. number on his smartphone, but angry to us „Its not my problem that you are not able to send me a message“

Facilities were old but in function. It was more a kittenette than a kitchen, only to electrical hot plates. Directly close to a busy, noisy street.

Cost-benefit ratio of this campsite is bad - too expensive for this benefits some others are way better and cheaper e.g. in the nationalpark.

I would not recommend this campsite!
Stayed the one night, plenty of shade, close to town.  Would recommend.

Awesome site.
Close to town
Great & friendly staff
Okay für eine Nacht aber teuer... 50$ für einen Wohnwagenstellplatz. Der Park hat leider seine besten Jahre hinter sich und punktet eigentlich nur durch nahe Lage. Sauber, naja.

A caravan park a little away from the hustle and bustle of town centre, near to the industrial hub.  A little dated but marvellous large sites. Excellent amenities.  Management is excellent! Couldn’t help enough.  Nice and spacious.

If you want real value for money, you can't go past Acclaim Pine Grove Holiday Park. Their Pay for 3 stay for 4 nights deal is the best in Esperance, Western Australia.

I stayed on a powered site in my motorhome.

This caravan park is great. Initially I came for four days and stayed for a month. Apart from a bit of traffic noise which I don't hear because my van is well insulated, this park amongst the trees is unassuming and tranquil.
The staff have become more like family over my stay here.
The facilities are being improved and upgraded.
I definitely highly recommend staying here for an extended getaway.

Just came back from a spontaneous getaway, loved the cleanliness of the cabin and the stress free nature and ease of it, staff were amazing and very helpful, definitely an under reviewed accommodation, is on a perfect location is quiet and close to all your needs. Will definitely stay and be booking again.

Just came back from lovely weekend down in esperance with the family, love staying in the two bedroom cabin was great for the dramatic climate changes we endure during our stay 😂. Staff are super friendly and accommodating, the cabin was spotless with everything we needed for a totally stress free get away, And was in a great location quiet easy to get to. Close to everything. 100 percent will be booking again, thank you again
Nice place

Park is very welcoming... nice shade and grass areas. Good location..easy push bike ride to the beach.. No Pool as advertised. Great for couples, kids playground needs an upgrade as do all amenities. Lots of road noise from the main road but we had a nice stay..

This caravan park is old and in need of a lot of upgrades, especially the amenities block. The sites are quite uneven and hard to get your van level to run the fridge. This park would be a last resort place to stay as there are better parks around the area.

We stayed in a cabin due to bad| weather. The couple at the office  were pleasant and helpful. We required a warm and comfortable place after camping out in rain. That's exactly what we got. The cabin was dated, but, it was warm, comfortable and had everything we needed. The park itself is under winter maintenance.  Out all day, only required the evenings. Nice people.

We stayed here christmas holidays 2023 a group of 8 cars for 7 powered sites stayed for 6 days

Day1 of our stay the 2 bathroom and toilet was blocked smells and not flushing. We could smell it from the inside of the tents
Day 2 was when all the drains in the powered site over flowed of human faeces urine and t@mpons. Our big group with tents and swag had to help each other out to move our staff away from the dirty water.
At the end the owners never helped us not even a discount as we all had to squeeze in 2 campsite. When we asked them if we could have at least an compensation bc of what happened safety risks owners offered told us " ur more than welcome to leave the park" has been advised by owners that it has been an on going issue with the camp site. The shouldn'thave operated if thats the case.
So we ended up paying full price of 7 sites for 6 days even though we only squeezed in 2 campsites for 5days
RUDE!
Not even an apology, clearly they just dont care.
Pool wasnt working.

After reading a lot of these negative comments, l see we have a lot of Karen’s whinging over trivial things. After staying there ourselves, there was absolutely nothing wrong with the place if your a caravan user or tent camper. Facilities are fine. Only recommendation would be don’t camp near the road, camp up the back because of the road noise (tent user) To all the negative Karens out there, you may as well go to a motel, because you’re not a camper.

We stayed two nights in our tent. Fantastic hospitality, super lovely people. Facilities were great, clean and easy to use. It’s an older style park, very homely feel to it. Nice grassy areas and small play ground. If you love the simple things in life this is it. Highly  recommended you give this place a go.
Thanks for the great hospitality 😊

After reading some of these reviews we booked our stay with some trepidation...but we were pleasantly surprised! The park is located well for sightseeing and town centre.  Our site was a flat, concrete slab with plenty of room for our car. Lots of trees and grass. I think the pool has been retired!
The amenities are simple but clean... fully functional laundry and plenty of clothes line.
The staff are friendly and welcoming and the price was very reasonable.
Overall I would recommend this park.. not sure what people expect but we have certainly stayed in a lot worse caravan parks!

We recently enjoyed a delightful 3 night stay at Acclaim Pine Grove Holiday Park, staying in a cosy chalet that was perfect for our family of two adults and two kids. The chalet was very clean and tidy, which made our stay all the more comfortable. On a lighter note, we found it quite amusing that the TV remote was missing—apparently, the previous guests had lost it :)
Overall, it was a wonderful visit and we'd love to return.

We needed a place to stay. Had car problems. The management was fantastic. So helpful and the park was getting full quite often. They found us two nice spots with an ensuite looking at the bush with lots of birds. Thanks for helping and being so accommodating
The most run down caravan park we have stayed in in WA.
The cabin was very old and haven’t renovated yet. The lights were not working and there was no exhaust in the bathroom. Aircon was not working.
Nice place, older style cabin but still okay.
I found this place to be nice. Clean amenities, friendly and helpful staff. We camped on their worst site right next to the road and had no issues. We really enjoyed it here.
All photos need to be updated there's no pool and the cabins are revolting myself and friends stayed in 30,31 cabin totally full of black mould under kitchen sink should be condemned. Total rip off
Stayed there last week in a basic room which was fine. However advertised is wifi and a pool and neither are available which then makes cost to high as not closest to town you can get.

Staff are really nice. Very tranquil setting.  Caravan sites are large and clean. Bathrooms and laundry are spacious and clean. Park needs a big update including a sound proof fence for traffic noise. Would stay again.

Such a sad camp site. We stayed last year and said never again but when everywhere is full you have too. It was expensive, run down and lots of facilities broken. So sad to see esperance decline like this.
Read the comments Acclaim, fix it or shut it, respect your managers at this place

Firstly to the Management running the park, great job, wonderful, friendly, welcoming. The park definitely needs a face-lift. We stayed in cabin 32, clammy, smelly, damp, wet, mould in bathroom, small bathroom. If you are over weight you will go through the shower floor and you won't fit on the toilet. Tight squeeze between 2 walls. The bed needs replacing. My husband and I kept sagging in the middle. Grounds are well maintained and watered and looked after but I do believe the owners need new cabins and need to spend money on the park. This is a holiday destination and Esperance is beautiful. Don't make life harder for your Management if you are to greedy to out lay money. Definitely not worth $160.00 per night. Maybe $60.
Pool - empty. Toilets ran out of toilet paper. Grounds are in poor condition. Would not stay again.
Very accommodating staff. Good size ensuite. Nice and private site.

We stayed one night. 2 x Adults, no pets. We just needed a place to park our van while exploring this beautiful coast. Power plus water $48.  We loved this park. The sites are spacious. The reception person was more than accommodating, very polite and friendly. The amenities are old. They are very clean. The pool is not operational at present. There is a tv/relaxation area. There are plenty of dvds to choose from or books to read. Our site was dirt. We have a lovely mat, so we are OK with this. There are a couple of swings for the children. Also, there is a  laundry and  large washing lines. The park felt very peaceful and well shaded. It is on a main highway. We are not light sleepers, so this did not affect us. We had great telstra reception, but the tv reception was a bit hit and miss.

The receptionist has been kind and friendly on our walk-in booking. The park is dog friendly. It’s beside the highway so it get bit noisy during the night with the trucks running. It’s very old and needs a good makeover.The amenities only have 2 showers and toilets for the whole park. We paid $48 Friday night in Feb2024.
Very dissatisfied  not clean at all terrible toilets  and showers needs a lot of work

I won’t be going back to the Acclaim Pine Grove Holiday Park in Esperance ever again – thankfully.  We had pre-paid to stay for 5 days.  After setting up the van, we found the stench of the open sewerage pit a few metres from the caravan made us all feel ill.  We asked reception to put some kind of a cover on the pit. The male manager/owner wandered down and partially covered the pit with a piece of corrugated iron.  It didn’t stop the sewerage stench continually flowing over the campsite. The manager said it had been like that for around 2 months. He said he couldn’t get tradespeople.  I strongly suggested it was an urgent WHS issue and needed to be covered before someone fell into the confined space of the pit. He argued that he was a health and safety rep and it was fine. He also told me he was a police inspector in New Zealand, and the sewerage pit was safe. The pictures I’ve attached don’t show a sewerage pit made safe – sadly. Management know it’s an issue as they have surrounded it with plastic tape.
I’ve also included pictures of the ladies toilets – the tap is sitting on the bench and will be for who knows how long. Ladies toilets and showers are putrid. In our 5 days stay, we noticed they were cleaned once. The males in our group had to contend with an overflowing urinal – please check out the pictures, and excrement on the walls next to the toilet paper. The pool is out of order and the pool fence is falling down. One side of the pool fence is being held up by chicken wire, and the gate is falling off – totally unsafe.  Some of the small units are completely closed with “no entry” signs on them and they are boarded up with tape.
The laundry has a machine that is turned the wrong way around, the recreation room has a layer of thick dust covering everything – it’s desperately in need of a clean. Grab a facemask before you walk in.
Oh, and before I forget, don’t leave your campsite without a torch at night as the majority of the night lights don’t work – surprise!  This park is shockingly part of a group called Acclaim.  I give it one star out of 20 as the location is not too bad.

The park is very old and needs a good makeover. The grounds themselves are reasonably well maintained, but the advertised swimming pool was 'out of action indefinitely', the camp kitchen was small, and the one-way roads through the park are quite tight when towing the van through. The park is right beside the highway, and if you're camped near the front of the park, it can get a bit noisy at night, especially if you're in a campervan or roof top tent. The amenities only have 2 showers and toilets for the whole park (some sites with ensuite). These are very old but clean. They also have a laundry. There is no dump point available, but there is a public dump point approx. 2km from the park, which is easily accessible and has water available to clean your cassette. The park is dog 🐕 friendly and the managers and staff are very friendly.  The park is in the industrial area of Esperance and only a couple of minutes from town. We paid $38/night in Feb 2024.

Very disappointing park, very very rundown only 2 toilets and 2 showers in mens and ladies block,no dump point pool been out of service for over a year bins overflowing with rubbish raw sewage smell from toilets.
Overall very overpriced for what you get we are on a powered site right next to busy road roadtrains constantly driving past very poorly maintained park little to zero maintenance office staff never there always closed never ever coming back park should be condemned extremely outdated and very unsafe..
Great place to camp. We'll definitely be going there again.

When booking, we were not informed that the campsite is located right next to a major road with no barrier or wall in between.

During the late hours of the night, there were speeding vehicles and noisy road trains, making it impossible to get a good night's sleep. This resulted in a dangerously drowsy state while driving long distances the next day.

Additionally, it seems that there were some vagrants wandering around the campsite, and we are unsure of where they came from.

Acclaim Pine Grove Esperance, where do we start….

This place needs to be investigated not only by the health department, but every other safety and other depart out there.

The cabin my partner's parents are staying in, has all out of date appliances and non tagged, a portable electric stove on top of a broken oven/stove instead of removing it,  Which is filthy both inside the small oven, and on the back/outside, hasn't been cleaned EVER.
The cupboards are full of Dust and mouse poo.
Window stills are falling apart.
Light globes are blown.
The toilet smells like sewage when you flash.

Further more, The ensuite site we are on,
Was filthy, the  shower was full of sand when we arrived, the walls covered in white soap scum, no effort in scrubbing, to get the place clean.
We bleached the entire ensuite prior to using ANYTHING And again the toilet smells like sewage when you flush it.

The entire park is run down, unsafe and unhealthy, it needs to be closed down and majorly overhauled or better off demolishing and starting again.

AVOID THIS PARK
This park is extremely run down and the pictures online are very old from when it used to be maintained , the on-site sewage system needs a complete overhaul where some campers on one side had to be moved as raw sewage was leaking out and into there sites , the water for there toilets has a horrible stench accroos the whole site so there bore water isn't deep enought or they have a major issue with leaching sewage, other friends stayed in a cabin  with 30 plus year old appliances that were completly unusable and unsanitary which nothing had even been tested or tagged a big fire hazard waiting to happen, the cabins are very run down with Rat or mice feces all through the cupboards and the stench of the toilet smelt through the whole cabin , the whole park has been basicly let go happy to take everyone's money cause there's no where else to go but not willing to maintain the park and kept up to standards even being owned by a major company.

The toilets when flushed smell like raw sewage as it uses groundwater it is disgusting.|Although the town was lovely the caravan park was run down and basically a dump. They told us that the pool was being renovated however no one was there working on it and it wasn’t under construction or anything so we believe that they take no pride in the maintenance of their park. My family and i agreed that if we ever come back to esperance we should never ever come back to this dump they call a caravan park.

Pretty good place to stay, room is clean. Not too happy with the shower, the temperature is hard to get right, and the floor/bottom of the shower is creaky and just makes me uncomfortable in general. Nevertheless, a nice place.
Great allways has room for me.
The south African family that was managing the place were lovely. Overall the facilities were quite run down and their was no facilities near the unpowered sites which was annoying.
Really friendly very clean well worth staying here
I’ve been here multiple times and it is a very nice place
Retro opgelapt vakantie huisje buiten de stad. Park heeft dringend opknapbeurt nodig
Cabin had a foul smell. Sewerage problem?

Owners not maintaining toilets, not flushing properly, no urinal flush tank at all. 2 office ladies tired of situation. 1 plumber in town does caravan parks?  $48/n  . Got poor reception. They were not happy
Only book this place if you don’t have any other options left in Esperance.

I booked this accommodation 3 months ago for 3 families and paid nearly 50% deposit. Prior to arrival I called and make sure our booking is okay.
She demanded full pay before she handed room. I have given rest of the payment and told they lady that we will arrive bit late.
When we arrived from Perth it was 7 pm. She was very annoyed and saying ‘you people are very inconsiderate’ . Also in point she treated if she want she can cancel our booking and send us back .
Nice park older but comfortable  bush surrounds,  Managers really good people ,

I don't recommend this place at all after the lady on the phone was so incredibly rude. I arrived in Esperance late (about 9 PM) on a very wet evening and asked about accommodation. She replied "do you know what time it is?" and then hung up on me. I was seriously at risk of being trapped out in the cold on a miserable night (fortunately I found a nice place to stay). Absolutely rude and has no business even running such a business, and I sure hope she doesn't treat her family the way she treated me.

Booked in for Christmas period. Never got any confirmation from them. I have booked different property after waiting for couple of weeks.

And now after months they called me for payment. I explained them that I never got confirmation. So, I booked some other property. Staff were so rude and just hung up.

What am I supposed to do if I never got the confirmation email? If they got my email ID wrong it is not my mistake.
The lady was helpful but the place we stayed at is falling apart. It's old and needs a site uplift.
